title: Lesson 48 — The Traveler

words.definition: traveling from place to place, especially for work
words.example: An itinerant preacher moved from town to town each season.

words.definition: traveling from place to place, especially working or based in various places
words.example: A peripatetic musician who lived out of a single suitcase.

words.definition: a temporary stay in a place
words.example: After a brief sojourn in Rome, they continued south.

words.definition: a person who wanders from place to place without a home
words.example: He lived like a vagabond, sleeping wherever the road ended.

words.definition: living the life of a group with no permanent home, moving from place to place
words.example: Their nomadic ancestors followed the herds each spring.

words.definition: a strong desire to travel and explore the world
words.example: College left her with a permanent case of wanderlust.
Their cross-continent odyssey took the better part of a year.

words.definition: a journey to a sacred place, or one made for a meaningful personal reason
words.example: Fans make an annual pilgrimage to the stadium where it began.

words.definition: a journey undertaken by a group with a particular purpose
words.example: The expedition set out to map the unexplored coastline.

words.definition: a brief excursion or initial attempt into a new activity or area
words.example: Her first foray into painting produced a surprising canvas.

words.definition: a short journey or trip, especially for pleasure
words.example: They planned a day excursion to the nearby caves.

words.definition: to wander at random without a fixed course or purpose
words.example: The trail meanders along the river for miles.

story: Old Teodor had spent his whole life [[itinerant|itinerant]], a truly [[peripatetic|peripatetic]] fiddler who never stayed anywhere long. A month here, a [[sojourn|sojourn]] there — some called him a [[vagabond|vagabond]], others simply a man of [[nomadic|nomadic]] habit, driven by pure [[wanderlust|wanderlust]]. His life had become one long [[odyssey|odyssey]], part [[pilgrimage|pilgrimage]] and part accident. What began as a single [[expedition|expedition]] north turned into decades of small [[foray|forays]] into every province, each one really just a longer [[excursion|excursion]] than the last, his route always ready to [[meander|meander]] toward whatever town had music that week.
